name: mixed-research

applies_to.description: Applied research project where code, prose, and data coexist. Typical structure: src/ for executable code, docs/ for prose and reports, data/ for datasets and outputs, notebooks/ for Jupyter analysis notebooks, tests/ for automated tests. This template is intentionally ASK_USER-heavy because mixed projects have high per-file judgment requirements — the boundary between 'active experiment' and 'orphaned scratch work' is rarely clear from file path alone.

default_rules.note: All files under src/ are at their canonical location; src/ is the code area and its internal structure is the project's own convention — ORGANIZE does not reorganize within src/
Prose documents under docs/ are at their canonical location; docs/ is the prose area for reports, design notes, and documentation
All files under data/ are at their canonical location; data/ is the data area for datasets, raw inputs, and processed outputs — ORGANIZE does not reorganize within data/
Jupyter notebooks under notebooks/ are at their canonical location; notebooks/ is the analysis area for exploratory and reporting notebooks
Test files under tests/ are at their canonical location; includes Python tests, fixtures, and test data files
Standard root-level orientation files are canonically at project root; must evaluate before the loose-markdown rule to prevent misrouting

Loose Python files at project root (not in src/) are likely misplaced source code; MOVE_TO src/ — ASK_USER if the file looks like a one-off experiment or setup script that the author intentionally left at root (e.g., setup.py, conftest.py, manage.py); do not auto-move without user confirmation

Loose markdown at project root not matched by t6 — ASK_USER always; in a mixed research project, a root-level .md could be a report draft (should go to docs/), an active design note (should stay), or an orphaned memo (should go to .archive); per-file judgment is required

Loose data files at project root (CSV, JSON, Parquet, etc.) — ASK_USER always; could belong in data/ (dataset), could be a config file (should stay at root), or could be an experiment output that is no longer needed; content and naming context are required to decide

Files whose basename begins with 'tmp' or 'scratch' are temporary work artefacts; quarantine to .delete — always ASK_USER before ratifying in a research context, as scratch files sometimes contain unreplicated experimental results
Files and directories prefixed old_*, backup/, or v<N>/ are historical snapshots; ASK_USER — likely .archive but research projects may retain these as experiment baselines; user decides

notes: Mixed research template. This template is intentionally ASK_USER-heavy by design: research projects have high per-file judgment requirements that rule patterns alone cannot capture. The boundary between 'active experiment' and 'orphaned scratch work' is rarely clear from file path alone — file content and authorial context are often required to decide correctly. Expect FLAG_NEW_RULE to surface project-specific patterns over time; this is by design. Users may need to add large dataset directories to ignore_paths (e.g., 'data/raw/**' if raw data is too large to enumerate). Rule t7 (loose .py at root → src/) includes an important caveat: setup.py, conftest.py, manage.py, and similar framework convention files are intentionally at root — TRIAGE must ASK_USER rather than auto-move when the filename suggests it could be a convention file.
